nyheter.title: ‘Alpha’ troops and more ships: Acting Navy Secretary Hung Cao outlines vision for service
nyheter.summary: In one of his first public addresses as acting Navy secretary, Hung Cao said he didn't need "cross-dressers" or "leaf eaters" in the service.

nyheter.title: Navy Awards $282.9M FF(X) Frigate Contract to HII’s Ingalls Shipbuilding
nyheter.summary: The Navy issued HII’s Ingalls Shipbuilding a $282.9 million contract award Tuesday to do lead yard work for the new frigate based on the Coast Guard’s National Security Cutter. A Pentagon announcement noted that the Navy did not compete the contract and said the work should wrap up in April 2028. The award comes after former Navy Secretary John Phelan cancelled the Constellation-class frigate program last year and opted to pursue a new frigate based on the NSC Legend-class hull built at Ingalls.

nyheter.title: Amphib USS San Antonio Returns from Southern Command After More Than 8-month Deployment
nyheter.summary: NAVAL STATION NORFOLK, Va. — One third of the amphibious force that has patrolled U.S. Southern Command since late August returned Tuesday to its homeport of Norfolk, Va. Amphibious warship USS San Antonio (LPD-17) moored at pier 9 at Naval Station Norfolk to a waiting crowd of families after 257 days. San Antonio departed in August as part of the Iwo Jima Amphibious Ready Group along with big-deck amphib USS Iwo Jima (LHD-7) and Flight II San Antonio-class amphib USS Fort Lauderdale (LPD- 28).

nyheter.title: Cao says industry needs to ‘move forward’ in first speech as acting SecNav
nyheter.summary: Acting Secretary of the Navy Hung Cao delivered his first public remarks since stepping into the role.

nyheter.title: Russian, Chinese Warships Sail Through Japan’s Southwest Region to East China Sea
nyheter.summary: Russian and People’s Liberation Army naval groups made separate transits over the past week through Japan’s southwest region to enter the East China Sea, according to officials from the island country. The Japan Maritime Self-Defense Force (JMSDF) implemented a major organizational reform at the end of March 2026. nyheter.title: What we know about the US military’s new joint laser weapon system
nyheter.summary: The Army-Navy effort aims to produce a containerized 150-kilowatt high-energy laser weapon to counter incoming cruise missiles.

nyheter.title: Amphibs Bougainville, Fallujah Deliveries Are Pushed Another Year
nyheter.summary: The next two big-deck amphibious warships will take about nine years each to build, as the Navy pushes the planned delivery date for the vessels by another year, USNI News has learned. The future USS Bougainville (LHA-8) will now deliver to the Navy in July of 2027 instead of August of 2026, according to the Fiscal Year 2027 budget justification documents. The service cited “construction performance indicators and shipyard labor challenges” as the reason for the delay. The future USS Fallujah (L

nyheter.title: Navy Pushes MQ-25A Stingray IOC Back to 2029 while Production Aircraft Takes First Flight
nyheter.summary: The first unmanned tankers for the U.S. Navy won’t be ready to deploy on aircraft carriers until 2029, according to Navy budget documents. The Boeing MQ-25A Stingray, the unmanned carrier tanker developed to extend the range of the air wing, will reach initial operational capability three years later than the Navy planned, according to budget documents reviewed by USNI News. nyheter.title: US Navy’s unmanned MQ-25A Stingray notches first successful test flight
nyheter.summary: The U.S. Navy and Boeing conducted the Stingray's first test flight on Saturday, showing its ability to taxi, take off, fly and land autonomously.

nyheter.title: U.S. Considering Foreign Designs, Shipyards for New Navy Frigate, Destroyer Work in $1.85B Study
nyheter.summary: American officials are considering foreign designs and having U.S. warship components built in overseas yards as part of an expansive manufacturing study proposed in the Fiscal Year 2027 budget, USNI News has learned. To expand naval shipbuilding capacity for U.S. warships, the FY 2027 budget submission includes $1.85 billion in research and development funding for a pair of studies on building future foreign frigate and destroyer designs, according to budget documents reviewed by USNI News. nyheter.title: Middle East Shipping ‘Paralyzed’ by Dueling U.S., Iranian Blockades, Analysts Say
nyheter.summary: U.S. and Iranian forces are both interdicting ships as the two countries grapple with control of the flow of maritime traffic through the region, leaving the shipping industry ‘paralyzed,’ experts say. In the past week, the U.S. interdicted three ships – two in the Indian Ocean and one in the Strait of Hormuz – while Iran went after three ships, seizing two. There are reports that the third ship was able to continue its journey. The U.S. and Iran also continue to trade barbs, over social media,

nyheter.summary:
On April 23, the Indonesian Navy (TNI AL) conducted a large-scale combat exercise in the Java Sea, featuring a sinking exercise (SINKEX) as part of a broader live-fire drill involving naval and air assets. This marks the first SINKEX since May 2024. The target vessel was the decommissioned Frosch-class landing ship tank (LST) KRI Teluk ...

nyheter.title: North Korea Test Launches Short Range Ballistic Missiles with Cluster Munitions
nyheter.summary: North Korea recently launched five short range ballistic missiles (SRBMs) armed with cluster munitions against an island target in the Sea of Japan for the second time this month, further trialing the warhead’s capabilities, state media reported Monday. North Korea’s Missile Administration conducted the test fire Sunday to evaluate the characteristics and power of the cluster bomb warhead and the fragmentation mine warhead applied to the surface-to-surface “Hwasongpo-11-Ra” missile, state media

nyheter.title: Navy to Integrate PAC-3 Missiles on Aegis Guided-Missile Destroyers
nyheter.summary: NATIONAL HARBOR, Md. — The Navy has finalized its decision to integrate Advanced Capability 3 Missile Segment Enhancements aboard its warships, Lockheed Martin announced this week. The sea service will integrate the PAC-3 MSEs with the service’s ubiquitous Mark 41 vertical launching systems and Aegis Combat System, both of which make up the backbone of U.S. naval air defense. nyheter.title: US Navy is reviewing cost of future Ford-class carriers to ensure they ‘make sense’
nyheter.summary: The service is looking at how superior Ford-class carriers are to Nimitz-class carriers, said the recently ousted Navy Secretary John Phelan.

nyheter.title: Australia to Increase Defense Spending by $37.9B Over Next Decade
nyheter.summary: Australia will increase its defense spending by $37.9 billion U.S. dollars over the next decade, according to its 2026 National Defense Strategy and accompanying Integrated Investment Program documents, which were unveiled last week. nyheter.summary: The two services will work together to boost the country's amphibious fleet size and availability after the readiness rate of its ships dropped in 2025.

rov_teknologi.title: Navy Tests Long-Range Munition Capable of Maritime Strike, Mining Missions
rov_teknologi.summary: NATIONAL HARBOR, Md. — The U.S. Navy tested a new guided munition capable of striking naval targets or deploying sea mines from aircraft hundreds of miles away, service officials revealed Monday. Naval Air Systems Command (NAVAIR) disclosed the first tests of the Joint Direct Attack Munition (JDAM) Long Range (LR), classified as the GBU-75, on Monday during the Navy League’s Sea-Air-Space symposium.
